verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचयतिर्ययाति: संयातिरायतिर्वियति: कृति: ।षडिमे नहुषस्यासन्निन्द्रियाणीव देहिन: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācayatir yayātiḥ saṁyātirāyatir viyatiḥ kṛtiḥṣaḍ ime nahuṣasyāsannindriyāṇīva dehinaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O King Parīkṣit, as the embodied soul has six senses, King Nahuṣa had six sons, named Yati, Yayāti, Saṁyāti, Āyati, Viyati and Kṛti.

verses.sanskrit: राज्यं नैच्छद् यति: पित्रा दत्तं तत्परिणामवित् ।यत्र प्रविष्ट: पुरुष आत्मानं नावबुध्यते ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājyaṁ naicchad yatiḥ pitrādattaṁ tat-pariṇāmavityatra praviṣṭaḥ puruṣaātmānaṁ nāvabudhyate
verses.text: When one enters the post of king or head of the government, one cannot understand the meaning of self-realization. Knowing this, Yati, the eldest son of Nahuṣa, did not accept the power to rule, although it was offered by his father.

verses.sanskrit: पितरि भ्रंशिते स्थानादिन्द्राण्या धर्षणाद्द्विजै: ।प्रापितेऽजगरत्वं वै ययातिरभवन्नृप: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitari bhraṁśite sthānādindrāṇyā dharṣaṇād dvijaiḥprāpite ’jagaratvaṁ vaiyayātir abhavan nṛpaḥ
verses.text: Because Nahuṣa, the father of Yayāti, molested Indra’s wife, Śacī, who then complained to Agastya and other brāhmaṇas, these saintly brāhmaṇas cursed Nahuṣa to fall from the heavenly planets and be degraded to the status of a python. Consequently, Yayāti became the king.

verses.sanskrit: चतसृष्वादिशद् दिक्षु भ्रातृन् भ्राता यवीयस: ।कृतदारो जुगोपोर्वीं काव्यस्य वृषपर्वण: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: catasṛṣv ādiśad dikṣubhrātṝn bhrātā yavīyasaḥkṛta-dāro jugoporvīṁkāvyasya vṛṣaparvaṇaḥ
verses.text: King Yayāti had four younger brothers, whom he allowed to rule the four directions. Yayāti himself married Devayānī, the daughter of Śukrācārya, and Śarmiṣṭhā, the daughter of Vṛṣaparvā, and ruled the entire earth.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचब्रह्मर्षिर्भगवान् काव्य: क्षत्रबन्धुश्च नाहुष: ।राजन्यविप्रयो: कस्माद् विवाह: प्रतिलोमक: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācabrahmarṣir bhagavān kāvyaḥkṣatra-bandhuś ca nāhuṣaḥrājanya-viprayoḥ kasmādvivāhaḥ pratilomakaḥ
verses.text: Mahārāja Parīkṣit said: Śukrācārya was a very powerful brāhmaṇa, and Mahārāja Yayāti was a kṣatriya. Therefore I am curious to know how there occurred this pratiloma marriage between a kṣatriya and a brāhmaṇa.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचएकदा दानवेन्द्रस्य शर्मिष्ठा नाम कन्यका ।सखीसहस्रसंयुक्ता गुरुपुत्र्या च भामिनी ॥ ६ ॥देवयान्या पुरोद्याने पुष्पितद्रुमसङ्कुले ।व्यचरत्कलगीतालिनलिनीपुलिनेऽबला ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaekadā dānavendrasyaśarmiṣṭhā nāma kanyakāsakhī-sahasra-saṁyuktāguru-putryā ca bhāminī
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: One day Vṛṣaparvā’s daughter Śarmiṣṭhā, who was innocent but angry by nature, was walking with Devayānī, the daughter of Śukrācārya, and with thousands of friends, in the palace garden. The garden was full of lotuses and trees of flowers and fruits and was inhabited by sweetly singing birds and bumblebees.

verses.sanskrit: ता जलाशयमासाद्य कन्या: कमललोचना: ।तीरे न्यस्य दुकूलानि विजह्रु: सिञ्चतीर्मिथ: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tā jalāśayam āsādyakanyāḥ kamala-locanāḥtīre nyasya dukūlānivijahruḥ siñcatīr mithaḥ
verses.text: When the young, lotus-eyed girls came to the bank of a reservoir of water, they wanted to enjoy by bathing. Thus they left their clothing on the bank and began sporting, throwing water on one another.

verses.sanskrit: वीक्ष्य व्रजन्तं गिरिशं सह देव्या वृषस्थितम् ।सहसोत्तीर्य वासांसि पर्यधुर्व्रीडिता: स्त्रिय: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vīkṣya vrajantaṁ giriśaṁsaha devyā vṛṣa-sthitamsahasottīrya vāsāṁsiparyadhur vrīḍitāḥ striyaḥ
verses.text: While sporting in the water, the girls suddenly saw Lord Śiva passing by, seated on the back of his bull with his wife, Pārvatī. Ashamed because they were naked, the girls quickly got out of the water and covered themselves with their garments.

verses.sanskrit: शर्मिष्ठाजानती वासो गुरुपुत्र्या: समव्ययत् ।स्वीयं मत्वा प्रकुपिता देवयानीदमब्रवीत् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: śarmiṣṭhājānatī vāsoguru-putryāḥ samavyayatsvīyaṁ matvā prakupitādevayānīdam abravīt
verses.text: Śarmiṣṭhā unknowingly put Devayānī’s dress on her own body, thus angering Devayānī, who then spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: अहो निरीक्ष्यतामस्या दास्या: कर्म ह्यसाम्प्रतम् ।अस्मद्धार्यं धृतवती शुनीव हविरध्वरे ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho nirīkṣyatām asyādāsyāḥ karma hy asāmpratamasmad-dhāryaṁ dhṛtavatīśunīva havir adhvare
verses.text: Oh, just see the activities of this servant-maid Śarmiṣṭhā! Disregarding all etiquette, she has put on my dress, just like a dog snatching clarified butter meant for use in a sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: यैरिदं तपसा सृष्टं मुखं पुंस: परस्य ये ।धार्यते यैरिह ज्योति: शिव: पन्था: प्रदर्शित: ॥ १२ ॥यान् वन्दन्त्युपतिष्ठन्ते लोकनाथा: सुरेश्वरा: ।भगवानपि विश्वात्मा पावन: श्रीनिकेतन: ॥ १३ ॥वयं तत्रापि भृगव: शिष्योऽस्या न: पितासुर: ।अस्मद्धार्यं धृतवती शूद्रो वेदमिवासती ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yair idaṁ tapasā sṛṣṭaṁmukhaṁ puṁsaḥ parasya yedhāryate yair iha jyotiḥśivaḥ panthāḥ pradarśitaḥ
verses.text: We are among the qualified brāhmaṇas, who are accepted as the face of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The brāhmaṇas have created the entire universe by their austerity, and they always keep the Absolute Truth within the core of their hearts. They have directed the path of good fortune, the path of Vedic civilization, and because they are the only worshipable objects within this world, they are offered prayers and worshiped even by the great demigods, the directors of the various planets, and even by the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the Supersoul, the supreme purifier, the husband of the goddess of fortune. And we are even more respectable because we are in the dynasty of Bhṛgu. Yet although this woman’s father, being among the demons, is our disciple, she has put on my dress, exactly like a śūdra taking charge of Vedic knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: एवं क्षिपन्तीं शर्मिष्ठा गुरुपुत्रीमभाषत ।रुषा श्वसन्त्युरङ्गीव धर्षिता दष्टदच्छदा ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ kṣipantīṁ śarmiṣṭhāguru-putrīm abhāṣataruṣā śvasanty uraṅgīvadharṣitā daṣṭa-dacchadā
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: When thus rebuked in cruel words, Śarmiṣṭhā was very angry. Breathing heavily like a serpent and biting her lower lip with her teeth, she spoke to the daughter of Śukrācārya as follows.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मवृत्तमविज्ञाय कत्थसे बहु भिक्षुकि ।किं न प्रतीक्षसेऽस्माकं गृहान् बलिभुजो यथा ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: ātma-vṛttam avijñāyakatthase bahu bhikṣukikiṁ na pratīkṣase ’smākaṁgṛhān balibhujo yathā
verses.text: You beggar, since you don’t understand your position, why should you unnecessarily talk so much? Don’t all of you wait at our house, depending on us for your livelihood like crows?

verses.sanskrit: एवंविधै: सुपरुषै: क्षिप्‍त्वाचार्यसुतां सतीम् ।शर्मिष्ठा प्राक्षिपत् कूपे वासश्चादाय मन्युना ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ-vidhaiḥ suparuṣaiḥkṣiptvācārya-sutāṁ satīmśarmiṣṭhā prākṣipat kūpevāsaś cādāya manyunā
verses.text: Using such unkind words, Śarmiṣṭhā rebuked Devayānī, the daughter of Śukrācārya. In anger, she took away Devayānī’s garments and threw Devayānī into a well.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यां गतायां स्वगृहं ययातिर्मृगयां चरन् ।प्राप्तो यद‍ृच्छया कूपे जलार्थी तां ददर्श ह ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyāṁ gatāyāṁ sva-gṛhaṁyayātir mṛgayāṁ caranprāpto yadṛcchayā kūpejalārthī tāṁ dadarśa ha
verses.text: After throwing Devayānī into the well, Śarmiṣṭhā went home. Meanwhile, King Yayāti, while engaged in a hunting excursion, went to the well to drink water and by chance saw Devayānī.

verses.sanskrit: दत्त्वा स्वमुत्तरं वासस्तस्यै राजा विवाससे ।गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणिमुज्जहार दयापर: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dattvā svam uttaraṁ vāsastasyai rājā vivāsasegṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇimujjahāra dayā-paraḥ
verses.text: Seeing Devayānī naked in the well, King Yayāti immediately gave her his upper cloth. Being very kind to her, he caught her hand with his own and lifted her out.

verses.sanskrit: तं वीरमाहौशनसी प्रेमनिर्भरया गिरा ।राजंस्त्वया गृहीतो मे पाणि: परपुरञ्जय ॥ २० ॥हस्तग्राहोऽपरो मा भूद् गृहीतायास्त्वया हि मे ।एष ईशकृतो वीर सम्बन्धो नौ न पौरुष: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ vīram āhauśanasīprema-nirbharayā girārājaṁs tvayā gṛhīto mepāṇiḥ para-purañjaya
verses.text: With words saturated with love and affection, Devayānī said to King Yayāti: O great hero, O King, conqueror of the cities of your enemies, by accepting my hand you have accepted me as your married wife. Let me not be touched by others, for our relationship as husband and wife has been made possible by providence, not by any human being.

verses.sanskrit: यदिदं कूपमग्नाया भवतो दर्शनं मम ।न ब्राह्मणो मे भविता हस्तग्राहो महाभुज ।कचस्य बार्हस्पत्यस्य शापाद् यमशपं पुरा ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yad idaṁ kūpa-magnāyābhavato darśanaṁ mamana brāhmaṇo me bhavitāhasta-grāho mahā-bhujakacasya bārhaspatyasyaśāpād yam aśapaṁ purā
verses.text: Because of falling in the well, I met you. Indeed, this has been arranged by providence. After I cursed Kaca, the son of the learned scholar Bṛhaspati, he cursed me by saying that I would not have a brāhmaṇa for a husband. Therefore, O mighty-armed one, there is no possibility of my becoming the wife of a brāhmaṇa.

verses.sanskrit: ययातिरनभिप्रेतं दैवोपहृतमात्मन: ।मनस्तु तद्गतं बुद्ध्वा प्रतिजग्राह तद्वच: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yayātir anabhipretaṁdaivopahṛtam ātmanaḥmanas tu tad-gataṁ buddhvāpratijagrāha tad-vacaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: Because such a marriage is not sanctioned by regular scriptures, King Yayāti did not like it, but because it was arranged by providence and because he was attracted by Devayānī’s beauty, he accepted her request.

verses.sanskrit: गते राजनि सा धीरे तत्र स्म रुदती पितु: ।न्यवेदयत्तत: सर्वमुक्तं शर्मिष्ठया कृतम् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: gate rājani sā dhīretatra sma rudatī pituḥnyavedayat tataḥ sarvamuktaṁ śarmiṣṭhayā kṛtam
verses.text: Thereafter, when the learned King returned to his palace, Devayānī returned home crying and told her father, Śukrācārya, about all that had happened because of Śarmiṣṭhā. She told how she had been thrown into the well but was saved by the King.

verses.sanskrit: दुर्मना भगवान् काव्य: पौरोहित्यं विगर्हयन् ।स्तुवन् वृत्तिं च कापोतीं दुहित्रा स ययौ पुरात् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: durmanā bhagavān kāvyaḥpaurohityaṁ vigarhayanstuvan vṛttiṁ ca kāpotīṁduhitrā sa yayau purāt
verses.text: As Śukrācārya listened to what had happened to Devayānī, his mind was very much aggrieved. Condemning the profession of priesthood and praising the profession of uñcha-vṛtti [collecting grains from the fields], he left home with his daughter.

verses.sanskrit: वृषपर्वा तमाज्ञाय प्रत्यनीकविवक्षितम् ।गुरुं प्रसादयन् मूर्ध्ना पादयो: पतित: पथि ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vṛṣaparvā tam ājñāyapratyanīka-vivakṣitamguruṁ prasādayan mūrdhnāpādayoḥ patitaḥ pathi
verses.text: King Vṛṣaparvā understood that Śukrācārya was coming to chastise or curse him. Consequently, before Śukrācārya came to his house, Vṛṣaparvā went out and fell down in the street at the feet of his guru and satisfied him, checking his wrath.

verses.sanskrit: क्षणार्धमन्युर्भगवान् शिष्यं व्याचष्ट भार्गव: ।कामोऽस्या: क्रियतां राजन् नैनां त्यक्तुमिहोत्सहे ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṣaṇārdha-manyur bhagavānśiṣyaṁ vyācaṣṭa bhārgavaḥkāmo ’syāḥ kriyatāṁ rājannaināṁ tyaktum ihotsahe
verses.text: The powerful Śukrācārya was angry for a few moments, but upon being satisfied he said to Vṛṣaparvā: My dear King, kindly fulfill the desire of Devayānī, for she is my daughter and in this world I cannot give her up or neglect her.

verses.sanskrit: तथेत्यवस्थिते प्राह देवयानी मनोगतम् ।पित्रा दत्ता यतो यास्ये सानुगा यातु मामनु ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathety avasthite prāhadevayānī manogatampitrā dattā yato yāsyesānugā yātu mām anu
verses.text: After hearing Śukrācārya’s request, Vṛṣaparvā agreed to fulfill Devayānī’s desire, and he awaited her words. Devayānī then expressed her desire as follows: “Whenever I marry by the order of my father, my friend Śarmiṣṭhā must go with me as my maidservant, along with her friends.”

verses.sanskrit: पित्रादत्तादेवयान्यै शर्मिष्ठासानुगातदा ।स्वानां तत् सङ्कटं वीक्ष्य तदर्थस्य च गौरवम् ।देवयानीं पर्यचरत् स्त्रीसहस्रेण दासवत् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitrā dattā devayānyaiśarmiṣṭhā sānugā tadāsvānāṁ tat saṅkaṭaṁ vīkṣyatad-arthasya ca gauravamdevayānīṁ paryacaratstrī-sahasreṇa dāsavat
verses.text: Vṛṣaparvā wisely thought that Śukrācārya’s displeasure would bring danger and that his pleasure would bring material gain. Therefore he carried out Śukrācārya’s order and served him like a slave. He gave his daughter Śarmiṣṭhā to Devayānī, and Śarmiṣṭhā served Devayānī like a slave, along with thousands of other women.

verses.sanskrit: नाहुषाय सुतां दत्त्वा सह शर्मिष्ठयोशना ।तमाह राजञ्छर्मिष्ठामाधास्तल्पे न कर्हिचित् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: nāhuṣāya sutāṁ dattvāsaha śarmiṣṭhayośanātam āha rājañ charmiṣṭhāmādhās talpe na karhicit
verses.text: When Śukrācārya gave Devayānī in marriage to Yayāti, he had Śarmiṣṭhā go with her, but he warned the King, “My dear King, never allow this girl Śarmiṣṭhā to lie with you in your bed.”

verses.sanskrit: विलोक्यौशनसीं राजञ्छर्मिष्ठा सुप्रजां क्‍वचित् ।तमेव वव्रे रहसि सख्या: पतिमृतौ सती ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: vilokyauśanasīṁ rājañcharmiṣṭhā suprajāṁ kvacittam eva vavre rahasisakhyāḥ patim ṛtau satī
verses.text: O King Parīkṣit, upon seeing Devayānī with a nice son, Śarmiṣṭhā once approached King Yayāti at the appropriate time for conception. In a secluded place, she requested the King, the husband of her friend Devayānī, to enable her to have a son also.

verses.sanskrit: राजपुत्र्यार्थितोऽपत्ये धर्मं चावेक्ष्य धर्मवित् ।स्मरञ्छुक्रवच: काले दिष्टमेवाभ्यपद्यत ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: rāja-putryārthito ’patyedharmaṁ cāvekṣya dharmavitsmarañ chukra-vacaḥ kālediṣṭam evābhyapadyata
verses.text: When Princess Śarmiṣṭhā begged King Yayāti for a son, the King was certainly aware of the principles of religion, and therefore he agreed to fulfill her desire. Although he remembered the warning of Śukrācārya, he thought of this union as the desire of the Supreme, and thus he had sex with Śarmiṣṭhā.

verses.sanskrit: यदुं च तुर्वसुं चैव देवयानी व्यजायत ।द्रुह्युं चानुं च पूरुं च शर्मिष्ठा वार्षपर्वणी ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yaduṁ ca turvasuṁ caivadevayānī vyajāyatadruhyuṁ cānuṁ ca pūruṁ caśarmiṣṭhā vārṣaparvaṇī
verses.text: Devayānī gave birth to Yadu and Turvasu, and Śarmiṣṭhā gave birth to Druhyu, Anu and Pūru.

verses.sanskrit: गर्भसम्भवमासुर्या भर्तुर्विज्ञाय मानिनी ।देवयानी पितुर्गेहं ययौ क्रोधविमूर्छिता ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: garbha-sambhavam āsuryābhartur vijñāya māninīdevayānī pitur gehaṁyayau krodha-vimūrchitā
verses.text: When the proud Devayānī understood from outside sources that Śarmiṣṭhā was pregnant by her husband, she was frenzied with anger. Thus she departed for her father’s house.

verses.sanskrit: प्रियामनुगत: कामी वचोभिरुपमन्त्रयन् ।न प्रसादयितुं शेके पादसंवाहनादिभि: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: priyām anugataḥ kāmīvacobhir upamantrayanna prasādayituṁ śekepāda-saṁvāhanādibhiḥ
verses.text: King Yayāti, who was very lusty, followed his wife, caught her and tried to appease her by speaking pleasing words and massaging her feet, but he could not satisfy her by any means.

verses.sanskrit: शुक्रस्तमाह कुपित: स्त्रीकामानृतपूरुष ।त्वां जरा विशतां मन्द विरूपकरणी नृणाम् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śukras tam āha kupitaḥstrī-kāmānṛta-pūruṣatvāṁ jarā viśatāṁ mandavirūpa-karaṇī nṛṇām
verses.text: Śukrācārya was extremely angry. “You untruthful fool, lusting after women! You have done a great wrong,” he said. “I therefore curse you to be attacked and disfigured by old age and invalidity.”

verses.sanskrit: श्रीययातिरुवाचअतृप्तोऽस्म्यद्य कामानां ब्रह्मन् दुहितरि स्म ते ।व्यत्यस्यतां यथाकामं वयसा योऽभिधास्यति ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-yayātir uvācaatṛpto ’smy adya kāmānāṁbrahman duhitari sma tevyatyasyatāṁ yathā-kāmaṁvayasā yo ’bhidhāsyati
verses.text: King Yayāti said, “O learned, worshipable brāhmaṇa, I have not yet satisfied my lusty desires with your daughter.” Śukrācārya then replied, “You may exchange your old age with someone who will agree to transfer his youth to you.”

verses.sanskrit: इति लब्धव्यवस्थान: पुत्रं ज्येष्ठमवोचत ।यदो तात प्रतीच्छेमां जरां देहि निजं वय: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti labdha-vyavasthānaḥputraṁ jyeṣṭham avocatayado tāta pratīcchemāṁjarāṁ dehi nijaṁ vayaḥ
verses.text: When Yayāti received this benediction from Śukrācārya, he requested his eldest son: My dear son Yadu, please give me your youth in exchange for my old age and invalidity.

verses.sanskrit: मातामहकृतां वत्स न तृप्तो विषयेष्वहम् ।वयसा भवदीयेन रंस्ये कतिपया: समा: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: mātāmaha-kṛtāṁ vatsana tṛpto viṣayeṣv ahamvayasā bhavadīyenaraṁsye katipayāḥ samāḥ
verses.text: My dear son, I am not yet satisfied in my sexual desires. But if you are kind to me, you can take the old age given by your maternal grandfather, and I may take your youth so that I may enjoy life for a few years more.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीयदुरुवाचनोत्सहे जरसा स्थातुमन्तरा प्राप्तया तव ।अविदित्वा सुखं ग्राम्यं वैतृष्ण्यं नैति पूरुष: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-yadur uvācanotsahe jarasā sthātumantarā prāptayā tavaaviditvā sukhaṁ grāmyaṁvaitṛṣṇyaṁ naiti pūruṣaḥ
verses.text: Yadu replied: My dear father, you have already achieved old age, although you also were a young man. But I do not welcome your old age and invalidity, for unless one enjoys material happiness, one cannot attain renunciation.

verses.sanskrit: तुर्वसुश्चोदित: पित्रा द्रुह्युश्चानुश्च भारत ।प्रत्याचख्युरधर्मज्ञा ह्यनित्ये नित्यबुद्धय: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: turvasuś coditaḥ pitrādruhyuś cānuś ca bhāratapratyācakhyur adharmajñāhy anitye nitya-buddhayaḥ
verses.text: O Mahārāja Parīkṣit, Yayāti similarly requested his sons Turvasu, Druhyu and Anu to exchange their youth for his old age, but because they were unaware of religious principles, they thought that their flickering youth was eternal, and therefore they refused to carry out their father’s order.

verses.sanskrit: अपृच्छत् तनयं पूरुं वयसोनं गुणाधिकम् ।न त्वमग्रजवद् वत्स मां प्रत्याख्यातुमर्हसि ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: apṛcchat tanayaṁ pūruṁvayasonaṁ guṇādhikamna tvam agrajavad vatsamāṁ pratyākhyātum arhasi
verses.text: King Yayāti then requested Pūru, who was younger than these three brothers but more qualified, “My dear son, do not be disobedient like your elder brothers, for that is not your duty.”

verses.sanskrit: श्रीपूरुरुवाचको नु लोके मनुष्येन्द्र पितुरात्मकृत: पुमान् ।प्रतिकर्तुं क्षमो यस्य प्रसादाद् विन्दते परम् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-pūrur uvācako nu loke manuṣyendrapitur ātma-kṛtaḥ pumānpratikartuṁ kṣamo yasyaprasādād vindate param
verses.text: Pūru replied: O Your Majesty, who in this world can repay his debt to his father? By the mercy of one’s father, one gets the human form of life, which can enable one to become an associate of the Supreme Lord.

verses.sanskrit: उत्तमश्चिन्तितं कुर्यात् प्रोक्तकारी तु मध्यम: ।अधमोऽश्रद्धया कुर्यादकर्तोच्चरितं पितु: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: uttamaś cintitaṁ kuryātprokta-kārī tu madhyamaḥadhamo ’śraddhayā kuryādakartoccaritaṁ pituḥ
verses.text: A son who acts by anticipating what his father wants him to do is first class, one who acts upon receiving his father’s order is second class, and one who executes his father’s order irreverently is third class. But a son who refuses his father’s order is like his father’s stool.

verses.sanskrit: इति प्रमुदित: पूरु: प्रत्यगृह्णाज्जरां पितु: ।सोऽपि तद्वयसा कामान् यथावज्जुजुषे नृप ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti pramuditaḥ pūruḥpratyagṛhṇāj jarāṁ pituḥso ’pi tad-vayasā kāmānyathāvaj jujuṣe nṛpa
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: In this way, O Mahārāja Parīkṣit, the son named Pūru was very pleased to accept the old age of his father, Yayāti, who took the youth of his son and enjoyed this material world as he required.

verses.sanskrit: सप्तद्वीपपति: सम्यक् पितृवत् पालयन् प्रजा: ।यथोपजोषं विषयाञ्जुजुषेऽव्याहतेन्द्रिय: ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sapta-dvīpa-patiḥ saṁyakpitṛvat pālayan prajāḥyathopajoṣaṁ viṣayāñjujuṣe ’vyāhatendriyaḥ
verses.text: Thereafter, King Yayāti became the ruler of the entire world, consisting of seven islands, and ruled the citizens exactly like a father. Because he had taken the youth of his son, his senses were unimpaired, and he enjoyed as much material happiness as he desired.

verses.sanskrit: देवयान्यप्यनुदिनं मनोवाग्देहवस्तुभि: ।प्रेयस: परमां प्रीतिमुवाह प्रेयसी रह: ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: devayāny apy anudinaṁmano-vāg-deha-vastubhiḥpreyasaḥ paramāṁ prītimuvāha preyasī rahaḥ
verses.text: In secluded places, engaging her mind, words, body and various paraphernalia, Devayānī, the dear wife of Mahārāja Yayāti, always brought her husband the greatest possible transcendental bliss.

verses.sanskrit: अयजद् यज्ञपुरुषं क्रतुभिर्भूरिदक्षिणै: ।सर्वदेवमयं देवं सर्ववेदमयं हरिम् ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: ayajad yajña-puruṣaṁkratubhir bhūri-dakṣiṇaiḥsarva-devamayaṁ devaṁsarva-vedamayaṁ harim
verses.text: King Yayāti performed various sacrifices, in which he offered abundant gifts to the brāhmaṇas to satisfy the Supreme Lord, Hari, who is the reservoir of all the demigods and the object of all Vedic knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मिन्निदं विरचितं व्योम्नीव जलदावलि: ।नानेव भाति नाभाति स्वप्नमायामनोरथ: ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: yasminn idaṁ viracitaṁvyomnīva jaladāvaliḥnāneva bhāti nābhātisvapna-māyā-manorathaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Lord, Vāsudeva, who created the cosmic manifestation, exhibits Himself as all-pervading, like the sky that holds clouds. And when the creation is annihilated, everything enters into the Supreme Lord, Viṣṇu, and varieties are no longer manifested.

verses.sanskrit: तमेव हृदि विन्यस्य वासुदेवं गुहाशयम् ।नारायणमणीयांसं निराशीरयजत् प्रभुम् ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: tam eva hṛdi vinyasyavāsudevaṁ guhāśayamnārāyaṇam aṇīyāṁsaṁnirāśīr ayajat prabhum
verses.text: Without material desires, Mahārāja Yayāti worshiped the Supreme Lord, who is situated in everyone’s heart as Nārāyaṇa and is invisible to material eyes, although existing everywhere.

verses.sanskrit: एवं वर्षसहस्राणि मन:षष्ठैर्मन:सुखम् ।विदधानोऽपि नातृप्यत् सार्वभौम: कदिन्द्रियै: ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ varṣa-sahasrāṇimanaḥ-ṣaṣṭhair manaḥ-sukhamvidadhāno ’pi nātṛpyatsārva-bhaumaḥ kad-indriyaiḥ
verses.text: Although Mahārāja Yayāti was the king of the entire world and he engaged his mind and five senses in enjoying material possessions for one thousand years, he was unable to be satisfied.
